steps.title: Identify the narrator's relationship to his sisters' activities
steps.content: The sisters spend their day playing dolls and giggling in the attic. The narrator says he “did not concern myself with those things”— indicating he deliberately stayed apart from their interests and activities.

steps.title: Apply the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
”Concern myself with” means to involve oneself in something or be interested in it.

Choice C, “Take any interest in,” directly expresses this meaning — the narrator is saying he didn't take interest in what his sisters did.

The other three choices either describe unrelated actions (Buy, Create) or introduce an emotion not present in the text (Feel anger).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
